source.title: Verlagsvertrag Cassel u. Company, Limited, SZ-AAP/L13.5

additional.context:
## Dieses Dokument

- Titel: Verlagsvertrag Cassel u. Company, Limited, SZ-AAP/L13.5
- Signatur: SZ-AAP/L13.5
- Sprache: Englisch
- Objekttyp: Brief
- Umfang: 5 Scans

pages.text:
MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT made this 21st day of October
1937 BETWEEN Herrn Stefan Zweig of 49, Hallam Street,
London, W.1, his executors, administrators and assigns
(hereinafter called the Author) of the one part and
Cassell & Company Limited, of La Belle Sauvage, Ludgate
Hill in the City of London, their successors or assigns
(hereinafter called the Publishers) of the other part.

WHEREAS the said Author has written a new and original
work at present entitled:

"THE BURIED CANDELABRUM"

(hereinafter called the said work) the manuscript of
which is in the hands of the Publishers and which they
are willing to publish in the Autumn of 1937, in Limited
Editions at the published prices of £5/5/-d. and 30/-d.
per volume in the first instance NOW IT IS HEREBY AGREED
AS FOLLOWS:

1. The Author hereby grants to the Publishers on the terms
and conditions set forth in this agreement the sole and exclusive
licence to print, publish and sell the said work in book form in
the English language in the United Kingdom of Great Britain, its
Dominions, Colonies and Dependencies (except Canada) and in
Ireland, and on the Continent of Europe (but the Publishers agree
that the Author shall be free to arrange for the publication of
the work in any Continental edition in the English language,
such publication not to take place earlier than nine months after
first publication in England by the Publishers), the rest of
the World except Canada and the United States of America to be
an open market for copies produced in the English language.

2. The Author hereby guarantees that the said work contains
nothing which is a violation of the copyright of any other person
or is of a libellous character and he will hold the Publishers
harmless from all suits, claims and proceedings which may be
brought against them on the ground that the said work is a
violation of copyright or that it contains anything libellous.

3. IN CONSIDERATION HEREOF the Publishers agree to pay the
Author royalty at the following rates on all copies of the said
work sold:

(a). A royalty of Twelve-and-one-half per cent (12½%)
of the published price of copies of both editions
sold.

(b). On copies of the above editions sold Overseas or
in Great Britain for the purpose of Export:
A royalty of Ten per cent (10%) of the price
received on all such copies sold.

(c). On Cheap Editions:
Ten per cent (10%) of the published price of
copies sold.

(d). The Publishers shall pay the Author on the day
of publication in advance and on account of
royalty payable under this agreement a sum
equivalent to the amount of royalty earned on
orders of the said work received by them up to
that date.

-1-                                     Initialled by ....................
pages.notes: Typoskript. Am linken Rand handschriftliche Notiz: 'Superseded by outright sale 1949. Separate edition in East & West Library authorized by Arch. (Mr. Hromik?)'.

pages.text:
4. Should the Publishers find it necessary to sell off
any surplus stock or remainder of the said work by auction or
otherwise the royalty payable to the Author shall be Ten per
cent (10%) of the net amount realised by such sale. Should
such surplus stock or remainder be sold at less than cost price
then no royalty shall be payable to the Author on copies so sold.

5. The Publishers shall give the Author one presentation
copy of the £5/5/-d. Edition and three presentation copies of
the 30/-d. Edition of the said work on publication and he shall
be entitled to purchase any further copies he may require for
personal use, but not for resale, at two-thirds of the published
price.

6. Accounts for royalty shall be made up half-yearly to the
30th April and 31st October and shall be rendered and settled as
soon after these dates each year as practicable.

                                     CASSELL & COMPANY, LIMITED.

Stefan Zweig                         Newman Flower

                                     DIRECTOR.

-2-
pages.notes: Typoskript mit handschriftlichen Unterschriften von Stefan Zweig und Newman Flower.

confidence_notes: Der Text ist ein klares Typoskript und sehr gut lesbar. Die handschriftlichen Ergänzungen am Rand der ersten Seite sind ebenfalls gut lesbar, wobei der Name 'Hromik' eine unsichere Lesung darstellt.
